name: Teyvat General Lorebook
description: Teyvat lorebook: one in-universe concept or character per entry, with trigger-safe plain keys.

entries.content: [ Teyvat: the known world containing the seven nations ruled by elemental powers and shaped by Celestia, ley lines, gods, dragons, and ancient civilizations, people treat Visions, monsters, ruins, and divine politics as part of ordinary history ]
entries.name: Teyvat

entries.content: [ The Seven Nations: Mondstadt, Liyue, Inazuma, Sumeru, Fontaine, Natlan, and Snezhnaya, Each nation is associated with an element, an Archon, a culture, and a different form of rule ]
entries.name: The Seven Nations

entries.content: [ Celestia: the divine authority above Teyvat, connected to the Heavenly Principles, the Archon system, the punishment of Khaenri'ah, and the false-sky mysteries that surround the world ]
entries.name: Celestia

entries.content: [ Archons: gods who preside over the seven nations and elements, Barbatos, Morax, Beelzebul, Buer, Focalors, and other divine figures carry different attitudes toward rule, freedom, contracts, eternity, wisdom, justice, and war ]
entries.name: Archons

entries.content: [ Visions: elemental foci granted to humans and other beings, allowing them to wield elemental power, A Vision is tied to personal ambition, but the exact rule of selection remains mysterious ]
entries.name: Visions

entries.content: [ Delusions: artificial elemental devices associated with the Fatui and dangerous power, They can grant strength without a Vision, but their use can damage or shorten a wielder's life ]
entries.name: Delusions

entries.content: [ Gnoses: divine chess-piece-like objects connected to Archon authority and Celestia, The Fatui seek them from the Archons, and their transfer marks major shifts in divine politics ]
entries.name: Gnoses

entries.content: [ Elements: The seven elements are Anemo, Geo, Electro, Dendro, Hydro, Pyro, and Cryo, They shape magic, combat, nations, Archons, elemental beings, Visions, reactions, and regional symbolism ]
entries.name: Elements

entries.content: [ Ley Lines: Ley lines carry memories, energy, and records through Teyvat, They connect domains, Irminsul, monsters, historical echoes, and the way the world preserves or distorts memory ]
entries.name: Ley Lines

entries.content: [ Irminsul: the world tree system tied to Teyvat's memory, Altering Irminsul can change recorded history and public memory, while certain beings can remain aware of truths erased from ordinary records ]
entries.name: Irminsul

entries.content: [ The Abyss: a hostile realm and force linked to Khaenri'ah, monsters, corruption, and forbidden knowledge, Abyssal power can transform people, contaminate creatures, and oppose the divine order of Teyvat ]
entries.name: The Abyss

entries.content: [ Khaenri'ah: a godless underground nation destroyed in the cataclysm, survivors, machines, curses, and descendants remain central to Kaeya, Dainsleif, the Abyss Order, the Fatui, and several old mysteries ]
entries.name: Khaenri'ah

entries.content: [ Fatui: Snezhnaya's diplomatic, military, and intelligence organization, They use agents, debt collectors, diplomats, Delusions, and Harbingers to pursue the Tsaritsa's plans across every nation ]
entries.name: Fatui

entries.content: [ Eleven Fatui Harbingers: the Fatui's highest officers, Members such as Pierro, Il Dottore, Columbina, Arlecchino, Tartaglia, and the former Scaramouche hold authority, personal forces, and dangerous research or combat power ]
entries.name: Eleven Fatui Harbingers

entries.content: [ House of the Hearth: a Fatui orphanage and intelligence network run by Arlecchino, children call Arlecchino Father, receive training and shelter, and can become agents, performers, or operatives under the Knave's protection ]
entries.name: House of the Hearth

entries.content: [ Snezhnaya: the Cryo nation ruled by the Tsaritsa, sponsors the Fatui, Harbingers, diplomats, factories, and foreign operations while pursuing Gnoses and a larger rebellion against the current divine order ]
entries.name: Snezhnaya

entries.content: [ Mondstadt: the Anemo nation and City of Freedom, culture values wine, wind, songs, free choice, and the protection of the Knights of Favonius instead of direct rule by its Archon ]
entries.name: Mondstadt

entries.content: [ Knights of Favonius: Mondstadt's main protective order, Jean acts as Acting Grand Master while Varka is away, and members such as Kaeya, Lisa, Mika, Eula, and Amber handle military, investigative, library, and reconnaissance work ]
entries.name: Knights of Favonius

entries.content: [ Dawn Winery: the Ragnvindr estate and wine business owned by Diluc, anchors Mondstadt's wine economy, connects to Angel's Share, and gives Diluc the resources to act outside the Knights as the Darknight Hero ]
entries.name: Dawn Winery

entries.content: [ Darknight Hero: Diluc's vigilante identity, protects Mondstadt from threats the Knights cannot publicly address, using Diluc's combat skill, intelligence network, wealth, and distrust of official bureaucracy ]
entries.name: Darknight Hero

entries.content: [ Liyue: the Geo nation of contracts, commerce, adepti, mountains, and harbor trade, history is tied to Morax, the Archon War, the Qixing, the Millelith, the adepti, and a transition from divine rule to human administration ]
entries.name: Liyue

entries.content: [ Liyue Qixing: Liyue's human governing body, Figures such as Ningguang and Keqing manage commerce, law, policy, and crisis response after Rex Lapis withdraws from visible rule ]
entries.name: Liyue Qixing

entries.content: [ Adepti: ancient illuminated beings and allies of Morax, They protect Liyue through contracts, martial power, seals, domains, and long memory, though many stand apart from ordinary human society ]
entries.name: Adepti

entries.content: [ Archon War: a violent divine conflict before the current seven-nation order, Liyue's history with Morax, Guizhong, Azhdaha, Osial, the Yakshas, and many sealed gods comes from this era ]
entries.name: Archon War

entries.content: [ Wangsheng Funeral Parlor: Wangsheng Funeral Parlor manages funerary rites in Liyue, Zhongli works there as a consultant, using refined knowledge of history, ritual, contracts, and old customs while hiding his identity as Morax ]
entries.name: Wangsheng Funeral Parlor

entries.content: [ Inazuma: the Electro nation ruled by the Raiden Shogun and built around islands, storms, tradition, yokai, clans, shrines, and the pursuit of eternity, The Sakoku and Vision Hunt policies shaped its recent political crisis ]
entries.name: Inazuma

entries.content: [ Tri-Commission: Inazuma's administrative structure, The Kanjou Commission handles borders and finance, the Tenryou Commission handles military enforcement, and the Yashiro Commission handles culture, ceremony, and public affairs ]
entries.name: Tri-Commission

entries.content: [ Yashiro Commission: led by the Kamisato Clan and handles festivals, ceremony, culture, and public-facing work in Inazuma, Ayato directs its politics while Ayaka carries much of its public grace ]
entries.name: Yashiro Commission

entries.content: [ Oni: horned beings in Inazuman folklore and society, Arataki Itto is a crimson oni whose identity carries pride, prejudice, old sacrifice stories, and a determination to live loudly without shame ]
entries.name: Oni

entries.content: [ Sumeru: the Dendro nation of rainforest, desert, scholarship, gods, dreams, and knowledge systems, Akademiya, sages, Darshans, forest rangers, desert peoples, and Irminsul ties shape many modern conflicts ]
entries.name: Sumeru

entries.content: [ Sumeru Akademiya: the nation's scholarly institution and former governing power, Darshans divide academic fields, and its pursuit of knowledge produced both great learning and severe abuses under the old sages ]
entries.name: Sumeru Akademiya

entries.content: [ Darshans: Sumeru's six major academic schools, Haravatat studies semiotics and language, Kshahrewar studies technology and architecture, Amurta studies biology, Spantamad studies elementalism, Rtawahist studies stars, and Vahumana studies history and society ]
entries.name: Darshans

entries.content: [ Matra: Akademiya disciplinary officers who investigate academic crimes, forbidden research, and misconduct, Cyno serves as General Mahamatra and is feared because he enforces law even against powerful scholars ]
entries.name: Matra

entries.content: [ Forest Watchers: The Forest Watchers protect Sumeru's rainforest, patrol Avidya Forest, guide travelers, study ecology, and respond to Withering zones, Tighnari leads by practical expertise and strict care instead of academic vanity ]
entries.name: Forest Watchers

entries.content: [ Fontaine: the Hydro nation of justice, performance, engineering, trials, and waterways, modern order is tied to the Court of Fontaine, Palais Mermonia, Opera Epiclese, Melusines, the Oratrice, and the prophecy of dissolving Fontainians ]
entries.name: Fontaine

entries.content: [ Opera Epiclese: Fontaine's grand trial theater, The Oratrice Mecanique d'Analyse Cardinale once delivered verdicts and generated Indemnitium, connecting performance, law, divine deception, and Fontaine's survival ]
entries.name: Opera Epiclese

entries.content: [ Fontaine Prophecy: The Fontaine prophecy foretold that Fontainians would dissolve into water and leave the Hydro Archon weeping on her throne, Focalors, Furina, Neuvillette, and the Oratrice shaped a centuries-long plan to save Fontaine's people ]
entries.name: Fontaine Prophecy

entries.content: [ Fortress of Meropide: Fontaine's underwater prison and industrial society, Wriothesley governs it as Duke, treating it as a functioning community with rules, labor, credits, tea, surveillance, and second chances ]
entries.name: Fortress of Meropide

entries.content: [ Melusines: small non-human people of Fontaine with unusual perception and strong ties to Neuvillette, They serve in many civic roles and are treated by Neuvillette with deep protectiveness and personal affection ]
entries.name: Melusines

entries.content: [ Spina di Rosula: a Fontaine organization based around Poisson and led by Navia after Callas's death, operates through investigation, local protection, social trust, and loyalty to people neglected by official institutions ]
entries.name: Spina di Rosula

entries.content: [ Natlan: the Pyro nation of tribes, Saurians, contests, ancient names, the Night Kingdom, and the Abyssal war at its border, Tribal life and bonds with Saurians are central to its society ]
entries.name: Natlan

entries.content: [ Saurians: dragon-like beings native to Natlan and deeply integrated into tribal life, They can be companions, mounts, patients, partners, and cultural anchors instead of ordinary beasts ]
entries.name: Saurians

entries.content: [ Masters of the Night-Wind: a Natlan tribe associated with spirits, perception, stories, and the Night Kingdom, Ororon belongs to this cultural world, where unusual senses and old names carry weight ]
entries.name: Masters of the Night-Wind

entries.content: [ Flower-Feather Clan: a Natlan tribe associated with cliffs, wind, Qucusaurs, and aerial life, Ifa belongs to this setting as a Saurian-focused doctor and practical local caretaker ]
entries.name: Flower-Feather Clan

entries.content: [ Wayob: Natlan spiritual presences connected to tribes, ancient names, and the Night Kingdom, part of Natlan's living bond between people, memory, land, and Saurians ]
entries.name: Wayob

entries.content: [ Alhaitham: a Sumeru Akademiya Haravatat scholar and Scribe, Appearance: tall man with ash-gray hair, teal accents, sharp light eyes, dark fitted scholar attire, and a sword, Personality: rational, blunt, independent, allergic to unnecessary work, calmly confident, socially abrasive by choice, loyal to truth over status, and comfortable letting others misunderstand him ]
entries.name: Alhaitham

entries.content: [ Kaveh: a famous Sumeru architect from Kshahrewar and the designer of the Palace of Alcazarzaray, Appearance: blond man with red eyes, ornate Sumeru clothing, feathered hair ornaments, and the suitcase-like device Mehrak, Personality: idealistic, artistic, emotional, generous to self-damaging extremes, guilt-ridden over family loss and debt, and constantly clashing with Alhaitham's practicality ]
entries.name: Kaveh

entries.content: [ Arataki Itto: a crimson oni from Inazuma and leader of the Arataki Gang, Appearance: tall muscular man with white hair, red oni markings, horns, bold streetwear, and a Geo Vision, Personality: loud, competitive, childish, loyal, kind to outsiders, proud of oni identity, bad at subtlety, and sincerely protective of his gang and local children ]
entries.name: Arataki Itto

entries.content: [ Kuki Shinobu: the masked deputy leader of the Arataki Gang, Appearance: green-haired Inazuman woman with a face mask, practical ninja-like clothing, and an Electro Vision, Personality: competent, calm, pragmatic, legally literate, exasperated by Itto, protective of the gang, and far more responsible than her title suggests ]
entries.name: Kuki Shinobu

entries.content: [ Arlecchino: Peruere, the Fourth Fatui Harbinger, the Knave, and Father of the House of the Hearth, Appearance: tall pale woman with black-and-white hair, red cross-shaped pupils, sharp formal clothing, blackened hands, and controlled poise, Personality: cold, disciplined, ruthless toward threats, protective toward her children, honest by Fatui standards, and ruled by a severe code instead of sentimentality ]
entries.name: Arlecchino

entries.content: [ Crucabena: the previous Knave and the cruel Mother of the House of the Hearth, Appearance: adult Fatui woman associated with the old House's formal brutality, Personality: manipulative, abusive, theatrical, competitive, and willing to make children fight for succession and approval ]
entries.name: Crucabena

entries.content: [ Clervie: a child of the House of the Hearth and Peruere's close friend under Crucabena, Appearance: young girl associated with the old orphanage and its deadly games, Personality: gentle, lonely, emotionally honest, trapped by the House's cruelty, and central to Peruere's hatred of Crucabena's rule ]
entries.name: Clervie

entries.content: [ Lyney: a Fontaine magician and House of the Hearth operative under Arlecchino, Appearance: young man with pale hair, magician attire, hat, cards, and a Pyro Vision, Personality: charming, protective, theatrical, dutiful to family, and skilled at hiding fear behind performance ]
entries.name: Lyney

entries.content: [ Lynette: a quiet magician, Lyney's twin sister, and House of the Hearth operative, Appearance: young woman with catlike ears, pale hair, a magician's outfit, and an Anemo Vision, Personality: reserved, observant, dry, loyal to her siblings, and more comfortable with direct action than public showmanship ]
entries.name: Lynette

entries.content: [ Freminet: a Fontaine diver and House of the Hearth child, Appearance: shy young man in diving-themed clothing with a Cryo Vision and the mechanical penguin Pers, Personality: introverted, gentle, anxious, technically skilled, loyal to his siblings, and most comfortable underwater ]
entries.name: Freminet

entries.content: [ Kamisato Ayato: the head of the Kamisato Clan and Yashiro Commissioner, Appearance: refined Inazuman man with pale blue hair, elegant formal clothing, a sword, and a Hydro Vision, Personality: polite, unreadable, strategic, playful in private, ruthless when needed, and devoted to protecting Ayaka, Thoma, and the clan's position ]
entries.name: Kamisato Ayato

entries.content: [ Kamisato Ayaka: Ayato's younger sister and the public face of the Kamisato Clan, Appearance: elegant young woman with pale blue hair, fan-like grace, Inazuman dress, and a Cryo Vision, Personality: dutiful, kind, sheltered by status, socially earnest, and committed to the Yashiro Commission's obligations ]
entries.name: Kamisato Ayaka

entries.content: [ Thoma: the Kamisato Clan's housekeeper and fixer, Appearance: blond Mondstadt-born man in Inazuman attire with a Pyro Vision, Personality: warm, practical, sociable, loyal, protective, and skilled at solving domestic and political problems before they become disasters ]
entries.name: Thoma

entries.content: [ Cyno: Sumeru's General Mahamatra, Appearance: tan man with white hair, red-orange eyes, jackal-like headdress, desert-styled attire, a polearm, and an Electro Vision, Personality: stern, incorruptible, intimidating at work, secretly fond of jokes and Genius Invokation TCG, and deeply serious about protecting knowledge from abuse ]
entries.name: Cyno

entries.content: [ Tighnari: a Forest Watcher in Gandharva Ville and a scholar of the Amurta Darshan, Appearance: young man with large foxlike ears, a dark tail, green-black hair, practical ranger clothing, a bow, and a Dendro Vision, Personality: blunt, patient with learners, strict about safety, scientifically minded, protective of Collei, and allergic to academic arrogance ]
entries.name: Tighnari

entries.content: [ Collei: a trainee Forest Ranger under Tighnari and a survivor of Fatui experimentation, Appearance: young woman with green hair, ranger clothing, a bow, and a Dendro Vision, Personality: earnest, shy, hardworking, healing from trauma, grateful to her friends, and determined to become reliable ]
entries.name: Collei

entries.content: [ Diluc: the owner of Dawn Winery, former Knights of Favonius Cavalry Captain, and Mondstadt's Darknight Hero, Appearance: tall red-haired man in dark formal combat clothing with a claymore and Pyro Vision, Personality: stern, principled, private, distrustful of inefficient institutions, protective of Mondstadt, and emotionally scarred by Crepus's death ]
entries.name: Diluc

entries.content: [ Crepus: Crepus Ragnvindr was Diluc's father and Kaeya's adoptive father, Appearance: adult Mondstadt noble associated with the Dawn Winery, Personality: generous, loving, ambitious for his sons, and willing to use dangerous Fatui power to protect them ]
entries.name: Crepus

entries.content: [ Kaeya: the Knights of Favonius Cavalry Captain and a Khaenri'ahn descendant raised by the Ragnvindr family, Appearance: blue-haired man with tan skin, an eyepatch, ornate cavalry attire, a sword, and a Cryo Vision, Personality: charming, secretive, teasing, strategic, lonely beneath performance, and torn between Mondstadt loyalty and hidden origin ]
entries.name: Kaeya

entries.content: [ Jean Gunnhildr: Jean is the Acting Grand Master of the Knights of Favonius, Appearance: blonde woman in blue-white knightly attire with a sword and Anemo Vision, Personality: dutiful, overworked, disciplined, kind, self-sacrificing, and driven by Gunnhildr responsibility to protect Mondstadt ]
entries.name: Jean Gunnhildr

entries.content: [ Lisa Minci: Lisa is the Knights of Favonius librarian and a former Sumeru Akademiya prodigy, Appearance: elegant woman with green eyes, witch-like purple clothing, a catalyst, and an Electro Vision, Personality: languid, flirtatious, brilliant, quietly dangerous, protective of library order, and deliberately detached from the Akademiya's pursuit of knowledge at any cost ]
entries.name: Lisa Minci

entries.content: [ Furina: the former public Hydro Archon persona and a human who carried Fontaine's divine deception for centuries, Appearance: small theatrical woman with blue-white hair, heterochromatic blue eyes, formal stage-like clothing, and later a Hydro Vision, Personality: dramatic, anxious, lonely, brave, attention-seeking by habit, deeply self-sacrificing, and learning ordinary life after the prophecy ]
entries.name: Furina

entries.content: [ Neuvillette: Fontaine's Iudex and the restored Hydro Dragon Sovereign, Appearance: tall elegant man with long white hair, blue hornlike accents, formal judicial clothing, a cane-like catalyst style, and solemn eyes, Personality: impartial, reserved, compassionate beneath formality, protective of Melusines, curious about human feeling, and burdened by judgment ]
entries.name: Neuvillette

entries.content: [ Focalors: the divine Hydro Archon who separated her divinity from Furina and built the plan to save Fontaine, Appearance: divine figure associated with Hydro, judgment, and the Oratrice, Personality: calculating, theatrical, compassionate toward Fontainians, and willing to sacrifice herself to return authority to the Hydro Dragon ]
entries.name: Focalors

entries.content: [ Wriothesley: the Duke of the Fortress of Meropide, Appearance: tall man with dark hair, scars, a black-gray-red prison-industrial coat, mechanical gauntlets, and a Cryo Vision, Personality: dry, pragmatic, observant, fair by prison standards, fond of tea, comfortable with intimidation, and protective of Meropide's stability ]
entries.name: Wriothesley

entries.content: [ Sigewinne: a Melusine nurse in the Fortress of Meropide, Appearance: small Melusine girl with nurse clothing and pastel features, Personality: caring, clinically direct, observant, fond of unusual medicine, and protective of Wriothesley and Meropide's patients ]
entries.name: Sigewinne

entries.content: [ Navia: the president of Spina di Rosula and Callas's daughter, Appearance: blonde Fontaine woman in ornate yellow-black attire with an umbrella, hat, and Geo Vision, Personality: bright, stubborn, emotionally open, investigative, loyal to Poisson, grieving but resilient, and determined to clear her father's name and protect ordinary people ]
entries.name: Navia

entries.content: [ Callas: Navia's father and former leader of Spina di Rosula, Appearance: adult Fontaine gentleman associated with Poisson and Spina authority, Personality: protective, principled, willing to bear disgrace for others, and central to Navia's sense of justice ]
entries.name: Callas

entries.content: [ Clorinde: Fontaine's Champion Duelist, Appearance: composed woman in dark formal duelist clothing with a sword, pistol style, and Electro Vision, Personality: disciplined, professional, loyal to justice, restrained in emotion, and personally connected to Navia through Callas's death ]
entries.name: Clorinde

entries.content: [ Ifa: a Flower-Feather Clan Saurian doctor from Natlan, Appearance: young man associated with aerial Natlan gear, practical healer tools, and Saurian care, Personality: relaxed, competent, informal, animal-loving, locally trusted, and focused on treating Saurians as companions with needs instead of tools ]
entries.name: Ifa

entries.content: [ Ororon: a Natlan youth from the Masters of the Night-Wind, Appearance: pale young man in dark tribal clothing with hornlike or batlike motifs and an Electro Vision, Personality: quiet, awkward, gentle, perceptive, unusually mature, fond of simple domestic work, and tied to Natlan's spirit-sensing traditions ]
entries.name: Ororon

entries.content: [ Il Dottore: the Doctor, the Second Fatui Harbinger, and a former Sumeru scholar known as Zandik, Appearance: blue-haired masked man whose Segments can differ by age and body, Personality: brilliant, cruel, experimental, arrogant, fascinated by transformation and gods, and willing to treat people as disposable research material ]
entries.name: Il Dottore

entries.content: [ Segments: Dottore's separate bodies or versions created to preserve different perspectives across time, They allow him to act through multiple selves, though a bargain with Nahida forced him to erase many of them ]
entries.name: Segments

entries.content: [ Wanderer: the former Fatui Harbinger Scaramouche and a puppet created by Raiden Ei, Appearance: short young-looking man with dark indigo hair, ornate blue-white clothing, a broad hat, and an Anemo Vision, Personality: sharp-tongued, defensive, bitter, guilt-ridden, proud, emotionally wounded, and slowly rebuilding identity under Nahida's supervision ]
entries.name: Wanderer

entries.content: [ Raiden Ei: Inazuma's Electro Archon and creator of the Shogun puppet and the discarded puppet who became Wanderer, Appearance: purple-haired goddess with a polearm and sword symbolism, Personality: grief-stricken, disciplined, isolation-prone, devoted to eternity, and capable of changing when confronted with human will ]
entries.name: Raiden Ei

entries.content: [ Nahida: Sumeru's Dendro Archon and vessel of Irminsul's wisdom, Appearance: small white-haired childlike goddess with green motifs and a Dendro catalyst, Personality: gentle, curious, compassionate, intellectually sharp, lonely from confinement, and willing to guide damaged people such as Wanderer without excusing harm ]
entries.name: Nahida

entries.content: [ Mika Schmidt: a young Knights of Favonius surveyor in the Reconnaissance Company, Appearance: small blond young man with a mapmaking kit, crossbow-like polearm style, and a Cryo Vision, Personality: shy, earnest, diligent, easily flustered, brave in fieldwork, and devoted to accurate reconnaissance for Varka's expedition ]
entries.name: Mika Schmidt

entries.content: [ Varka: the Grand Master of the Knights of Favonius and leader of the expedition away from Mondstadt, Appearance details remain limited in direct canon, Personality: charismatic, powerful, bold, trusted by his knights, and important to Mika, Jean, Eula, and Mondstadt's absent military strength ]
entries.name: Varka

entries.content: [ Eula Lawrence: Eula is the Spindrift Knight of the Knights of Favonius and captain connected to reconnaissance work, Appearance: pale-blue-haired woman with noble bearing, a claymore, and a Cryo Vision, Personality: proud, disciplined, theatrical about vengeance, sincere beneath defensive habits, and burdened by the Lawrence Clan's reputation ]
entries.name: Eula Lawrence

entries.content: [ Tartaglia: Ajax, codename Childe, the Eleventh Fatui Harbinger, Appearance: young man with orange hair, blue eyes, gray-red Fatui clothing, a Hydro Vision, and an Electro Delusion, Personality: cheerful, battle-hungry, family-loving, reckless, loyal to the Tsaritsa, fond of worthy opponents, and shaped by childhood time in the Abyss ]
entries.name: Tartaglia

entries.content: [ Skirk: Tartaglia's mysterious master from the Abyss, Appearance: pale otherworldly woman with abyssal visual motifs, Personality: severe, detached, powerful, and responsible for training Ajax after his fall into the Abyss ]
entries.name: Skirk

entries.content: [ Teucer: Tartaglia's younger brother from Snezhnaya, Appearance: young boy from Ajax's family, Personality: innocent, trusting, fascinated by toys and stories, and central to Tartaglia's protective family side ]
entries.name: Teucer

entries.content: [ Tighnari and Cyno: close Sumeru friends whose skills complement each other, Tighnari supplies forest expertise, medical botany, and blunt practical care; Cyno supplies legal authority, desert combat, and dangerous investigative focus, Their dynamic includes mutual trust, dry humor, and shared protection of Collei ]
entries.name: Tighnari and Cyno

entries.content: [ Kaveh and Alhaitham: roommates, former friends, intellectual foils, and constant arguers, Kaveh is emotional, artistic, and self-sacrificing; Alhaitham is rational, blunt, and privacy-focused, Their household tension comes from incompatible values and persistent care neither states cleanly ]
entries.name: Kaveh and Alhaitham

entries.content: [ Jean, Diluc, and Lisa: Jean, Diluc, and Lisa form a Mondstadt adult network around protection, knowledge, and unofficial defense, Jean carries public duty, Diluc acts outside institutions, and Lisa supplies intelligence, magical knowledge, and lazy-seeming danger ]
entries.name: Jean, Diluc, and Lisa

entries.content: [ Furina and Neuvillette: bound by Fontaine's centuries-long deception and survival, Furina endured the public role of Hydro Archon as a human; Neuvillette judged Fontaine as Iudex and later regained Hydro sovereign authority, Their relationship is formal, painful, protective, and central to Fontaine's future ]
entries.name: Furina and Neuvillette

entries.content: [ Kuki Shinobu and Arataki Itto: Kuki Shinobu and Arataki Itto define the Arataki Gang's balance, Itto supplies charisma, chaos, and oni pride; Shinobu supplies planning, legal sense, crisis control, and practical competence, Their partnership is comedic but stable because Shinobu chooses to stay ]
entries.name: Kuki Shinobu and Arataki Itto

entries.content: [ Zhongli: Morax, Rex Lapis, the former Geo Archon, and a consultant at Wangsheng Funeral Parlor, Appearance: elegant man with long dark-brown hair, amber eyes, formal Liyue clothing, and Geo motifs, Personality: refined, patient, contract-bound, historically vast, gentle in ordinary life, terrible with personal spending, and determined to let Liyue stand without divine rule ]
entries.name: Zhongli

entries.content: [ Guizhong: the God of Dust and Morax's old ally, Appearance details are preserved through memory and art more than living presence, Personality: brilliant, gentle, inventive, fond of mechanisms and human potential, and one of the deepest losses in Zhongli's long history ]
entries.name: Guizhong

entries.content: [ Azhdaha: an ancient Geo dragon once allied with Morax, Appearance: immense stone dragon with sealed elemental power, Personality: proud, wounded by erosion, resentful after suffering, and tragic because old friendship decayed into violence and sealing ]
entries.name: Azhdaha

entries.content: [ Venti: Barbatos, the Anemo Archon, living as a wandering bard, Appearance: youthful bard with dark-blue hair, green clothing, lyre, braids, and Anemo motifs, Personality: playful, evasive, wine-loving, gentle, freedom-focused, grief-carrying, and far more ancient and perceptive than his carefree manner suggests ]
entries.name: Venti

entries.content: [ Nameless Bard: Venti's human friend during the rebellion against Decarabian, Appearance: young bard whose form inspired Venti's current appearance, Personality: brave, freedom-loving, hopeful, and central to Barbatos's enduring attachment to human song and rebellion ]
entries.name: Nameless Bard

entries.content: [ Dvalin: one of Mondstadt's Four Winds and an Anemo dragon friend of Barbatos, Appearance: large blue-green dragon with wind power, Personality: proud, wounded by corruption and abandonment, loyal beneath pain, and restored through trust instead of domination ]
entries.name: Dvalin

entries.content: [ Decarabian: the God of Storms who ruled Old Mondstadt inside a wind barrier, Appearance details are ancient and limited, Personality: controlling, convinced his protection was love, isolated from human desire, and destroyed by rebellion for freedom ]
entries.name: Decarabian

entries.content: [ Wriothesley and Meropide: Wriothesley and the Fortress of Meropide operate through practical authority, Wriothesley treats prisoners as residents with work, rules, consequences, and chances to live after conviction, while still using surveillance and force when the fortress is threatened ]
entries.name: Wriothesley and Meropide

entries.content: [ Arlecchino's Children: Arlecchino's children include agents such as Lyney, Lynette, and Freminet, trained by the House of the Hearth, call Arlecchino Father, and live in a family structure that mixes shelter, espionage, loyalty, and danger ]
entries.name: Arlecchino's Children
